title: aws-sm: throttling/limit errors classified as Backend (permanent) instead of Unavailable (transient)
description: AWS SM ThrottlingException and InternalServiceError arrive via e.as_service_error() and are currently mapped to SecretError::Backend (permanent, callers must not retry). Both are transient — callers with retry-on-Unavailable logic will permanently fail on throttled requests instead of backing off. Fix: check svc.meta().code() for 'ThrottlingException' and 'InternalServiceError' and return Unavailable, similar to how aws-ssm handles AccessDeniedException. Contrast: aws-ssm (refactored in pass 1) uses granular code checks; aws-sm uses a blanket service-error → Backend mapping that is wrong for transient service errors.
